name: PR-CV 198 Penya de Sella Loop
name_original: Alt de la Penya de Sella (PR-CV 198) — Circular desde Sella
description: This challenging 5.8-mile circular hike from Sella offers panoramic views and a significant 2357 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Sella are found in the Alicante province of Spain, a region characterized by its valley trails and mountain ridges. The landscape offers a mix of challenging ascents and scenic paths through natural gorges. This area provides diverse hiking experiences, from routes along water features to trails offering panoramic views of the surrounding terrain. Best hiking trails around Sella The most popular hiking route is PR-CV 198 Penya de Sella Loop, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 12 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Waterfall Near Sella – Font Mayor Gorge loop from Sella, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This route explores the Font Mayor Gorge, offering views of a waterfall. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Arrant d'Irles, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Hiking around Sella is defined by valley trails, mountain ridges, and natural gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Sella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1100 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Sella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Is Sella, Spain, the same as the Sella Group in the Dolomites?
A: No, the hiking trails discussed here are located in Sella, a municipality in the Alicante province of Spain. This region is known for its distinct valley trails and mountain ridges, offering a different landscape and hiking experience compared to the Sella Group in the Italian Dolomites.

Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sella?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails available around Sella, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes through natural gorges, along mountain ridges, and trails with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Sella?
A: Hiking around Sella features a varied landscape, primarily characterized by valley trails and mountain ridges. You'll encounter paths through natural gorges, offering a mix of challenging ascents and scenic routes. The terrain provides diverse experiences, from routes along water features to trails with panoramic views.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Sella?
A: Yes, Sella offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10356403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Arrant d'Irles&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.6-mile (5.8 km) trail that can be completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes, leading through varied terrain.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Sella?
A: Yes, Sella provides challenging routes for experienced hikers. The region features trails with significant elevation gain and demanding ascents. There are 3 difficult routes available, offering a good test for those seeking a more strenuous adventure.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sella?
A: The routes in Sella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1100 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views, and the opportunities to explore natural gorges and mountain ridges.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Sella?
A: Yes, Sella offers several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e867409649/pr-cv-198-penya-de-sella-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-CV 198 Penya de Sella Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.8-mile (9.3 km) trail that provides a comprehensive experience of the area's landscape.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Sella?
A: Yes, you can find trails that feature waterfalls in Sella. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23524380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall Near Sella – Font Mayor Gorge loop from Sella&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.0-mile (8.1 km) path that explores the Font Mayor Gorge, offering views of a waterfall.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural monuments to see near Sella's hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area around Sella is rich in natural landmarks. You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/619834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabosa Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and unique formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/120805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Arches&lt;/a&gt;. These points of interest add to the scenic beauty of the hikes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes in the vicinity of Sella offer views of or pass by bodies of water. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, providing serene waterside scenery along your hike.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Sella?
A: The Alicante province generally enjoys a mild climate, making hiking in Sella pleasant for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the trails, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: Are there any facilities or points of interest like springs or fountains along the trails?
A: Yes, you can find facilities such as springs along some routes. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4228929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Molí&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4500177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de Partegat&lt;/a&gt; are highlights in the area that provide natural water sources.
tips: The Alt de Nono offers very nice views towards the valleys of the Marina Alta and with good visibility you can reach the beaches of the Costa Blanca!

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this circular route?
faqs.answer: The Alt de la Penya de Sella (PR-CV 198) is a challenging hike. While komoot estimates it takes around 4 hours and 12 minutes to complete, some sources suggest it can take up to 4 hours and 15 minutes, depending on your pace and experience. Be prepared for a demanding outing.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Alt de la Penya de Sella (PR-CV 198) trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and often demanding. You'll encounter mountain paths, rocky sections, and potentially slippery areas, especially around the 'Runar de la Mona'. The route includes steep ascents through ravines (barrancos), forest tracks, and some asphalted roads near Sella. Expect a zigzagging mountain path and a ridge walk near the summit.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is consistently graded as difficult or high difficulty and is not suitable for beginners. It requires good physical condition, prior hiking experience, and appropriate footwear due to its technical and demanding sections, including steep climbs and potentially tricky footing.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Alt de la Penya de Sella (PR-CV 198) hike?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Sella. While specific parking details aren't provided, you would typically look for parking within or on the outskirts of the village of Sella, which serves as the trailhead for this circular route.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: To avoid extreme heat and enjoy more pleasant conditions, the best times to hike this route are generally spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making the demanding ascents more manageable. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Alt de la Penya de Sella (PR-CV 198) trail?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, given the trail's high difficulty, rocky terrain, and potentially slippery sections, it is advisable to only bring dogs that are experienced hikers, well-conditioned, and comfortable with challenging mountain environments. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are well-hydrated.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to hike the Alt de la Penya de Sella (PR-CV 198) trail. It is a public access route.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular panoramic views of the Marina Baixa region, including mountains, valleys, and on clear days, even the Mediterranean coast. You'll get impressive vistas of Puig Campana and Aitana. The 'Alto de la Penya' itself is a natural viewpoint. You'll also pass by the Altet de Nono summit and may spot the 'Cueva Grande' (Large Cave) and 'Casa de D'Alt'.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Finestrat-Sella, el Calvari, and [CIMA AT12] Tudons trails.

faqs.question: What should I know about trail signage and navigation?
faqs.answer: While it is a PR (Sendero de Pequeño Recorrido) trail, it's important to note that there hasn't been recent quality control on its signage and maintenance. Therefore, hikers should plan properly, have adequate training and equipment, and not rely solely on trail signs for navigation. Bringing a reliable map or GPS device is highly recommended.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), layers of clothing, and a first-aid kit. A map or GPS device is crucial due to potential issues with trail signage. Hiking poles can also be very helpful on the steep and rocky sections.
